name: Horno Tejero and Boquerón Reservoirs Loop
name_original: Embalses de Horno Tejero y Boquerón — circular desde Cordobilla de Lácara
description: Jog the Horno Tejero and Boquerón Reservoirs Loop, a moderate 7.4-mile route with scenic water views and varied terrain near Cordobilla de L
region_intro: Jogging around Cordobilla De Lácara offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de San Pedro foothills, characterized by rolling hills, extensive olive groves, and Mediterranean forests. The landscape features oak and cork trees, typical thickets, and scrubland, providing varied natural settings. The Lácara River flows to the south, contributing to fertile lands, while the Embalse de Horno Tejero and Embalse del Boquerón reservoirs add waterside views to many running routes. Best jogging routes around Cordobilla De Lácara The most popular jogging route is Horno Tejero and Boquerón Reservoirs Loop, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 20 minutes to complete, offering views of both reservoirs. Another top favourite among local runners is Dolmen de Carmonita Loop, a moderate 6.5 miles (10.4 km) path. This route features the ancient Dolmen de Carmonita, providing a blend of natural scenery and historical interest. Local runners also love the Dolmen de Lácara Interpretive Trail, a 2.0 miles (3.2 km) trail leading through the vicinity of the significant megalithic construction, often completed in about 23 minutes. Jogging around Cordobilla De Lácara is defined by its Sierra de San Pedro foothills, the Lácara River, and scenic reservoirs, with routes offering options for easy, moderate, and more challenging running. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 5 runners have used komoot to explore Cordobilla De Lácara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Cordobilla De Lácara?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;6 diverse running routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Cordobilla De Lácara listed on komoot, offering a range of experiences from easy jogs to more challenging runs. These routes allow you to explore the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Cordobilla De Lácara?
A: The jogging trails in Cordobilla De Lácara feature diverse terrain, including rolling hills within the Sierra de San Pedro foothills, extensive olive groves, and Mediterranean forests. You'll encounter paths winding through oak and cork trees, typical thickets, and scrubland, with some routes offering views of the Lácara River and local reservoirs.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy running route. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282239247/dolmen-de-lacara-interpretive-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Lácara Interpretive Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 2.0 miles (3.2 km) path with minimal elevation, perfect for a gentle run or for those new to jogging.

Q: Are there more challenging running routes for experienced joggers?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, the region offers routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24527044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de San Blas – Puerto del Trampal loop from Carmonita&lt;/a&gt;. This route spans 21.6 km (13.4 miles) with over 360 meters of elevation gain, providing a demanding workout amidst scenic landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Cordobilla De Lácara?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280715399/horno-tejero-and-boqueron-reservoirs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horno Tejero and Boquerón Reservoirs Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282231583/la-nava-de-santiago-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Nava de Santiago Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, both offering varied scenery.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while jogging?
A: Jogging routes often pass by significant natural and historical landmarks. You can enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horno Tejero Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, explore the vicinity of the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6063861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Lácara&lt;/a&gt;, or even reach the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6748952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Estena Summit and Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there running routes that offer water views?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to the Embalse de Horno Tejero and Embalse del Boquerón reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280715399/horno-tejero-and-boqueron-reservoirs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horno Tejero and Boquerón Reservoirs Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering continuous views of both bodies of water, making for a refreshing running experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Cordobilla De Lácara?
A: Many rural paths and trails in Cordobilla De Lácara are generally suitable for dogs, especially those that traverse meadows and olive groves. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially near livestock or in protected natural areas, and to carry water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Cordobilla De Lácara?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the best times for jogging in Cordobilla De Lácara. The weather is milder, and the natural landscape, with its Mediterranean forest and olive groves, is particularly vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late evenings preferable, while winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Are there any historical sites along the running paths?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282239247/dolmen-de-lacara-interpretive-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Lácara Interpretive Trail&lt;/a&gt; takes you near the significant megalithic construction of the Dolmen de Lácara. Another route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1346065892/dolmen-de-carmonita-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Carmonita Loop&lt;/a&gt;, also features an ancient dolmen, blending natural beauty with historical interest.

Q: Is public transport available to access the jogging routes?
A: Cordobilla De Lácara is a rural area, and direct public transport options to the trailheads may be limited. It is generally recommended to use a personal vehicle to reach the starting points of most running routes, especially those outside the immediate town center.

Q: What kind of vegetation will I encounter on the trails?
A: The trails in Cordobilla De Lácara showcase typical Mediterranean vegetation. You'll jog through areas rich with oak and cork trees, various thickets, and scrubland. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6748952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Estena Summit and Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, is known for its abundance of cork oak, pine, lavender, and strawberry trees.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Horno Tejero and Boquerón Reservoirs Loop?
faqs.answer: Based on komoot's data, jogging this 11.9 km loop usually takes around 1 hour and 20 minutes. Your actual time may vary depending on your pace and any stops you make.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The loop features a mixed terrain, including sections of asphalt, natural paths, paved tracks, and unpaved tracks. You'll also encounter general paths and roads. While the slopes are generally gentle, some areas have more unevenness that demands firm footing.

faqs.question: Is the Horno Tejero and Boquerón Reservoirs Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as moderate for jogging. While the overall slopes are gentle, some segments require stable footing. A good level of physical fitness is recommended, especially for the 11.9 km distance.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque views of both the Horno Tejero and Boquerón reservoirs. The landscape around Horno Tejero features dry eucalyptus trees, while Boquerón is often encircled by reeds and cattails. You'll also see submerged holm oaks and underwater vegetation in creek mouths, contributing to the natural beauty.

faqs.question: Are there any specific viewpoints or landmarks along the loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, a notable point of interest is the Horno Tejero Reservoir, which offers views of Cordobilla de Lácara. Both reservoirs are also known for their fishing opportunities and the region holds historical interest.

faqs.question: Is the Horno Tejero and Boquerón Reservoirs Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly, offering varied terrain and natural surroundings for your companion. However, always ensure your dog is under control, especially around wildlife or other trail users, and check local regulations for leash requirements.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be enjoyed year-round, spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging. During these seasons, the natural scenery is often at its most vibrant, and the weather is less extreme than in summer or winter.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to jog the Horno Tejero and Boquerón Reservoirs Loop. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Horno Tejero and Boquerón Reservoirs Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop starts from Cordobilla de Lácara. You should be able to find parking within the municipality, close to the trailhead, though specific parking areas are not detailed in the available information.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: The trail is located in the municipality of Cordobilla de Lácara. You may find local amenities such as cafes or restaurants within the town itself, which could be a good option before or after your jog.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for jogging this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the mixed terrain and some uneven sections, appropriate footwear with good grip is recommended. Hydration is always important for a 11.9 km jog. Depending on the season, consider sun protection or layers for cooler weather.
